analyst_observations: With-skill achieves 100% pass rate across all evals vs 50.4% baseline — the skill's structured report template is the primary differentiator.
Baseline passes all correctness assertions (typecheck, tests, actual refactoring) — both versions do the code changes correctly. The skill's advantage is entirely in report quality and completeness.
The rename eval shows the biggest delta: with-skill found references in plan docs that baseline missed (no-old-references assertion failed for baseline).
Cost: ~57% more tokens and ~2.6x longer duration. The overhead comes from reading the knowledge graph, running before/after verification, and generating the structured report.
Non-discriminating assertions: typecheck-passes and tests-pass both pass 100% regardless of skill — these validate refactoring quality, not skill quality.
The split-serialize baseline naturally included breaking changes and import impact sections (6/8 pass) — structured file operations prompt Claude to think about consumers even without the skill.
